title: What are the different types of Dependency Injection supported by Spring?

answers.junior: Spring supports constructor, setter, and field injection. Constructor injection is best for required parts, setter for optional, and field is less recommended.
answers.middle: Constructor injection is preferred for mandatory dependencies and immutability. Setter injection allows optional dependencies. Field injection uses @Autowired on fields but is discouraged for testability.
answers.senior: Constructor injection is best practice for modern Spring, enabling clear contracts and easy testing. Setter injection is for optional dependencies. Field injection complicates testing and maintainability. XML-based injection is legacy.
answers.tricky: Constructor injection avoids circular dependencies and supports immutable beans. Setter/field injection can resolve circular references. Annotation-based injection (@Autowired, @Value) is standard; XML is legacy. Always prefer constructor injection for clarity and reliability.
